data.title: Gold could gain another 20% next year, Goldman Sachs' Struyven says
data.description:
Gold could climb to $4,900/oz late next year on increased central bank buying and interest rate reductions, but even a small push into diversification from retail investors could deliver significantly more upside, Goldman Sachs co-head of global commodities research Daan Struyven told Bloomberg.

"Since the freezing of Russia's central bank reserves in 2022, [emerging market] reserve managers got this big wakeup call that they need to diversify into gold, which is the only truly safe asset once you hold it in your domestic vaults," Struyven said in the interview, adding that Goldman economists currently forecast 75 basis points more of Fed cuts.

"The key intuition for the size of this [potential] price upside from private sector diversification is that the gold market is relatively small," Struyven said, noting that the market for gold ETFs was ~70x smaller than the market for U.S. Treasurys - "It's another reason why gold is our favorite long commodity recommendation."

Gold futures gained for the fourth straight session Wednesday on growing expectations that the Federal Reserve will lower interest rates next month.

The latest batch of delayed U.S. data showed retail sales rose modestly in September, and wholesale prices increased in line with expectations, while, "the prospect of a policy shift gained additional traction as Kevin Hassett, widely viewed as the frontrunner to become the next Fed chair, is seen as aligned with President Trump's support for lower borrowing costs," MUFG analyst Soojin Kim said in a note.

Traders are now pricing in a nearly 85% chance of a December rate cut, according to the FedWatch tool.

Front-month Comex gold (XAUUSD:CUR) for December delivery closed +0.6% to $4,165.20/oz, and front-month Comex December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended +3.8% to $52.916/oz, the highest settlement value for both precious metals since November 13.

data.title: Banco Macro reports 9M results
data.description:
• Banco Macro press release (BMA): 9M net income totaled Ps.176.7 billion. This result was 35% or Ps.95.2 billion lower than the result posted in the same period of last year.
  • Operating income (after G&A and personnel expenses) totaled Ps.1.03 trillion, 64% or Ps.1.84 trillion lower than in 9M24.

data.title: World Copper appoints Mark Lotz as CEO
data.description:
• World Copper (WCU:CA) announced on Wednesday the appointment of Mark Lotz as the CEO and President of the company, effective November 24, 2025. 
  • Lotz brings 30 years of experience in financial reporting, securities filings, mergers, corporate finance, tax consulting, and capital markets.

data.title: Regenxbio files $300M mixed shelf offering
data.description:
• Regenxbio (RGNX) has filed a shelf registration to offer up to $300M in securities.
  • The offering may include common stock, preferred stock, debt securities, warrants, rights, or units, in one or more combinations and tranches.

data.title: Springview announces 1-for-8 reverse share split effective December 2
data.description:
• Springview Holdings (SPHL) said its board has approved a 1-for-8 reverse split of its Class A ordinary shares, effective December 2, reducing outstanding shares to about 1,652,204 from 13,217,629.
  • The par value of Class A shares will rise to $0.0008 from $0.0001, while Class B shares are unaffected.
  • The company said the move is aimed at lifting its share price to retain its Nasdaq listing.
  • Fractional shares will be rounded up to the nearest whole share.
  • SPHL +4.80% after hours to $0.48.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Zoetis receives European approval for Lenivia for osteoarthritis in dogs
data.description:
• The European Commission has granted approval to Zoetis' (ZTS) Lenivia (izenivetmab) for the treatment of pain associated with osteoarthritis in dogs.
  • A single injection lasts three months. 
  • The European Medicines Agency's Committee for Veterinary Medicinal Products issued a positive opinion on Lenivia in October.

data.title: Boeing to build 96 AH-64E Apache Helicopters for Poland under $4.7 billion contract
data.description:
• Boeing (BA) will produce AH-64E Apache attack helicopters for international customers, including 96 for the Polish Armed Forces, under a Foreign Military Sales contract awarded by the U.S. Army valued at nearly $4.7 billion.
  • Poland's order represents the largest number of Apache aircraft ordered outside of the United States in the program's history.
  • With deliveries expected to begin in 2028, the Polish Ministry of National Defence (MND) is already training pilots and maintainers on the attack helicopter. The MND currently leases eight aircraft from the U.S. Army.
  • The local industry will play a key role in performing maintenance and support of the Apache fleet. Boeing will also establish training programs and help develop a composite laboratory.
  • BA +0.11% after hours to $187.13.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sleeping Giant Capital launches private placement of debenture units
data.description:
• Sleeping Giant Capital (SSX:CA)on Wednesday announced a non-brokered private placement offering of up to 350 debenture units at a price of $1,000 per debenture unit, for aggregate gross proceeds of up to $350,000.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 14, 2025.
  • Each debenture unit will be consist of one $1,000 principal amount secured convertible debenture and 20,000 common share purchase warrants to acquire one common share of the company at a price equal to $0.05 per share during a period of five years following the issuance date of the warrants.
  • The convertible debentures will bear interest at a rate of 12% per annum
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Invest Green announces $172.5M initial public offering
data.description:
• Invest Green (NASDAQ:IGAC.UN) announced on Wednesday the closing of its initial public offering of 17.25M units at a price of $10.00 per unit on November 26, 2025.
  • The offering included full exercising by the underwriters of their overallotment option to purchase an additional 2.25M units.
  • Total gross proceeds from the offering were $172.5M.
  • The units began trading on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol “IGACU” on November 25, 2025.
  • Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one right entitling the holder thereof to receive one-tenth of one Class A ordinary share upon the completion of an initial business combination.
  • A registration statement relating to the securities became effective on November 24, 2025.

data.title: Spire Global receives NYSE notice
data.description:
• Spire Global (NYSE: SPIR) stated on Wednesday that it had received notice on November 25, 2025, from the NYSE due to its failure to timely file its quarterly report on Form 10-Q for the quarter ended September 30, 2025, with the SEC.
  • The company is not in compliance with the NYSE’s continued listing standards as set forth in Section 802.01E of the NYSE listed company manual.
  • Following the rules, the company can regain compliance with this NYSE listing standard by filing the Form 10-Q with the SEC before May 19, 2026.
